page: Amazon Web Services c8i.large is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor operating at 3.9 GHz. The hardware allocation consists of 1 physical core with hyper-threading, providing 2 vCPUs. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, which equates to 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, alongside hardware support for Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). Local storage is not included, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a significant contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The instance achieves top-tier results in single-threaded operations, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2175.0 and a gzip compression speed of 34.3 GB/sec. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench text processing also demonstrate strong performance. Conversely, multi-core and memory-bound metrics are weak. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance falls into the bottom 10%, while Geekbench multi-core, Passmark CPU, and memory bandwidth tests both small and large block rank in the bottom 25%.

The primary tradeoff of the c8i.large is its low resource density, offering only 4.0 GB of RAM and 2 vCPUs, which limits its multi-threaded capacity and memory-intensive throughput. This resource profile makes it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require high single-threaded CPU speed but have low memory footprints. Appropriate workloads include lightweight single-threaded processing, text processing, basic web serving, and small-scale single-threaded utility scripts, whereas multi-threaded compilation, database operations, and large-scale LLM inference are constrained by the hardware limits.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz with 1 physical core and 2 vCPUs. The instance is configured with 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, a 480 MB L3 cache, and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, with no local storage. Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities, including top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench single-core scores. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are weak, with stress-ng multi-core performance in the bottom 10%. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for single-threaded tasks but presents clear limitations for multi-threaded or memory-heavy workloads. It is best suited for lightweight text processing, single-threaded utilities, and basic web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor, 2 vCPUs, and 4.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, delivering strong single-core but limited multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.large is a compute-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server delivering strong single-threaded performance and high-speed DDR5 memory for lightweight, CPU-focused utility workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz
4.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 7 GHz
Strong single-core performance in gzip compression and text processing
Weak multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmark results
No local storage and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and basic web serving
